The '''67th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 16 February 2014 at the [[Royal Opera House]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2013 in film|2013]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Baftas 2014: full list of winners|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2014/feb/16/baftas-2014-winners-full-list|access-date=17 February 2014|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|date=16 February 2014}}</ref> The nominations were announced on 8 January 2014 by actor [[Luke Evans (actor)|Luke Evans]] and actress [[Helen McCrory]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Entered Films List 2013/14 |url=https://static.bafta.org/files/film-1314-entered-films-list-2122.pdf |website=bafta.org |publisher=British Academy of Film and Television Arts |access-date=25 January 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bafta.org/film/awards/baftas-where-when,2345,BA.html|title=The BAFTA Film Awards in 2014. Where & When?|publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] (BAFTA)|date=21 December 2011|access-date=10 January 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news | title=Bafta nominations 2014: Gravity gets 11 nods |date=8 January 2014 | work=[[The Daily Telegraph]] | url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/baftas/10557754/Bafta-nominations-2014-Gravity-gets-11-nods.html|first=Martin |last=Chilton | access-date=10 January 2014}}</ref> Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bafta.org/film/awards/public-information,2348,BA.html|title=Film Awards Information|publisher=British Academy of Film and Television Arts (BAFTA)|date=23 January 2013|access-date=10 January 2014}}</ref>

''[[Gravity (2013 film)|Gravity]]'' won six of its eleven nominations, including [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] for [[Alfonso Cuarón]], [[BAFTA Award for Best British Film|Outstanding British Film]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Cinematography|Best Cinematography]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Film Music|Best Original Music]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Sound|Best Sound]], and [[BAFTA Award for Best Special Visual Effects|Best Special Visual Effects]].<ref name="bafta1">{{cite web|url=http://awards.bafta.org/award/2014/film |title=Film in 2014 | BAFTA Awards |publisher=Awards.bafta.org |access-date=17 February 2014}}</ref> ''[[12 Years a Slave (film)|12 Years a Slave]]'' won [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]] and [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]] for [[Chiwetel Ejiofor]].<ref name="bafta1"/> [[Cate Blanchett]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]] for ''[[Blue Jasmine]]'',<ref name="bafta1"/> [[Barkhad Abdi]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role|Best Actor in a Supporting Role]] for ''[[Captain Phillips (film)|Captain Phillips]]'', and [[Jennifer Lawrence]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|Best Actress in a Supporting Role]] for ''[[American Hustle]]''.<ref name="bafta1"/><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2014/feb/16/baftas-2014-winners-full-list |title=Baftas 2014: full list of winners | Film |newspaper=The Guardian |date=16 February 2014 |publisher=theguardian.com |access-date=17 February 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-26218678|title=Bafta Film Awards 2014: Full list of winners|work=BBC News|date=17 February 2014|access-date=17 February 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/film/2014/feb/16/gravity-biggest-winner-bafta-awards-baftas|title= Gravity hits the heights but 12 Years a Slave takes best film|date=16 February 2014|work=Guardian|access-date=17 February 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url =https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-26221196 |title= Gravity and 12 Years a Slave share glory|date=16 February 2014|work=BBC News|access-date=17 February 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.empireonline.com/news/story.asp?NID=40162|title= BAFTAs 2014: The Ceremony, As It Happened|date=16 February 2014|work=Empire Online|access-date=18 February 2014}}</ref>

The ceremony was broadcast on [[BBC One]] and [[BBC Three]]. It was hosted by [[Stephen Fry]], the ninth time he's hosted the ceremony.<ref name="BBC">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-26221196|title=Baftas: Gravity and 12 Years a Slave share glory|work=BBC News |date=16 February 2014 |access-date=18 Feb 2014}}</ref> The ceremony opened with a duet call "Heroes" from [[Tinie Tempah]] and singer [[Laura Mvula]].<ref name="BBC"/> Viewing figures were the lowest since [[63rd British Academy Film Awards|2010]], with 4.73 million watching the ceremony.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.barb.co.uk/whats-new/weekly-top-30|title=Weekly Top 30 Programmes|publisher=[[Broadcasters' Audience Research Board|BARB]]|access-date=18 February 2014}}</ref>
